reviews.review_text: If companies can terminate a game at anytime, then I should be allowed a refund at anytime.
BIGGEST SCAM!!!! We've paid money for the game and now, not even an offline version of it will remainf

Bring me back to launch day. The queue screens, the first steps on the beach, the sound of chopping trees in a world that felt alive. New World was truly amazing at its core, a game with so much soul and promise. It’s tragic how that potential was squandered. Rest in peace, New World — 2021 to 2026, you deserved better.

By far my favorite and best MMORPG I've played next to Runescape.

I played alpha, beta one and two (maxing my character each time), then launch and on and off last two years, loving every second of it through all its ups and downs. I've made good friends who I still talk to today and play other games with.

At launch was one of the first 5 people to hit 60 on our server. One of the top 3 healers of the server and remained at the top after the first merge. Maxed all weapons and trade skills. Collected all journals, every named item, beat all mutations lvl 10 before the changes, lead raids and 85% of the achievements. Lead my own company, held a territory through all the lag (if you know you know how bad it got in the beginning) Reekwater will forever be my home. Day and night this was all I thought about, it was an addiction. After a long break I picked it up again a few months after The Rise of the Angry Earth came out and luckily still had a few people who played that knew me and so I had the in for wars. Surprisingly I still outshined most healers. And now I'm back for one last run before it's gone.

It's sad to see it go but nothing good lasts forever and this game will always have a place in my heart. I wanted this to be a forever game like Runescape where I could just pick it up anytime and just play but that's not the case. Tbh I feel for all the newer players y'all were definitely robbed. Amazon did you dirty and you should be mad but the OGs knew this. There was a 5 year plan while amazon developed the platform to sell with no guarantee the game would continue after. Everyone buys games, plays them, and then never touches them again, this is no different they just did it to us before we did it to them. Either way I personally enjoyed the game and got my moneys worth. 

I will however be playing for the next 6 months to reminisce, catch up on any content I missed in the past year and attempt to get those last few achievements I have left. Will miss you New World and all your Beauty.

500 hours in this game. At one point New World genuinely felt like the future of MMOs. The atmosphere, combat, and friendships I made were some of the best I’ve had in gaming.

Sadly the game just wasn’t cared for properly after launch and now it feels mostly dead. Still one of the best MMO foundations I’ve played, which honestly makes its downfall even more disappointing.

Don’t regret the hours though.
What a shame to permanently shut down this game. It has so much potentials, and this is the end. Man, what can I say? Thank you for the good memories I guess.

What a joke this game was. After all that, and it comes to this sort of end, well such is the fate of all online service slop. Guess this shall be a reminder to avoid any sort of titles made from Amazon and affiliates.  See you on the flip side.
if companies can terminate a game at anytime, then I should be allowed a refund at anytime.
The reason Stop Killing Games is needed. Yet another corporation just abandoning a bought and payed for product and expecting consumers to suck it up. Nope

i loved the game and to see it go i wish they would stop killing games. make it a single player with play ran servers at least....... i really hope the stop killing games thing makes them do it if they dont plain on doin it them selves

Due to it coming to a close, 
Please avoid any and all future purchases of this product.
So ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ dumb they're shutting the game down. Jeff Bezos and Amazon can suck me.
We should get at least a 50% refund if they are just closing the game. Biggest gaming scam of the decade.

This honestly was the best game ive ever played, the people i met and friendships i made on here truly made it amazing, from the good times to the bad, the toxic times to the random nice players just trying to help. it was the best time for gaming for me and im truly sad to see it die off like this. what a major let down by amazon. keep on cooking chefs, -brodyreg out

Really sad. I bought it back in 2021 when it just released and had an amazing time. Really wanted to see how the game would develop and saw it had a lot of potential. Sadly, I felt like the game "died" rather quickly. Servers felt really imbalanced so I stopped playing. It is really sad to see all these people who spent hundreds and thousands of hours in the game just for Amazon to take the servers down. I am 100% sure that Amazon could have done something better than this, sadly greed rules humanity these days.
Games like this are the reason steam should stop letting games onto the platform if they don't have a way to fully refund players or keep the game going in an offline single player.

To Amazon game studios, and all other game dev studios.
Players will remember new world; how we paid for it, put hundreds of hours into it, and then you take it away completely, for flimsy reasons a few years later.
We will remember this with all of your other games.
Had a blast. It's not like Amazon couldn't afford to keep this game going, they just don't care. RIP. This is what happens when you buy a game from a souless corporation.

I recommend going to the amazon game studios page and ignoring the creator, none of their games will show up on your store page anymore then they can't pull this bull on you again. yes i got 100 hours out of the game and it was super fun but having the game ripped away from us and not caring about the fan base that has been loyal to the game is such an amazon move.

what an absolute show of incompetence. The first 2 weeks of launch was probably the most fun I have had with a video game ever. Just for the dev/amazon team to make wrong choice after wrong choice until they had to shut it down.

Thanks for letting me know that the game I purchased will be unplayable in a year. I am also very impressed by the continued efforts to satisfy the community by still letting us download and install the game after the permanent server shutdown. I look forward to spending many more hours on this game staring at the 'Connection Error' popup while 70GB of dead binaries occupy my SSD and remind me of the poor decision I made by buying the game at launch.

was a fun game when it came out, was gonna start playing again. good ol giant corporation greed shutting down servers next year after all these players still playing... The Cycle Frontier.. New World. RIP :(

This is a highly enjoyable game with great mechanics, and I personally would love to invest another 300+ hours into it. However, I cannot recommend it to anyone new. Amazon Games is discontinuing New World: Aeternum and shutting down the servers permanently on January 31, 2027. Since the game is already delisted and has a definitive end date, it is not worth investing your time or energy into starting fresh.

Fu Amazon for taking it down. Could easily be a great co-op game. Invest the year to optimize it for that instead of taking it down. Steam should force them to give us a full refund on everything, the game and in-game currency.
If companies can terminate a game at anytime, then I should be allowed a refund at anytime.

Had the best levelling experience in any MMO ever for me, so sad to see it go away. 
Game is going into a EoS so can't really recommend

Kinda sad about New World going away. I remember buying this as soon as it came out. It was fun, and the pvp was the best I've ever seen for a game like this. The structure was incredible. My main issue was the bots and lack of content late game. I found myself reinstalling the game with every major update, playing for about a month, then uninstalling. Other than that the game was great. It's a bitter sweet end to a game I have close to 700 hours playing in. It was a love/hate relationship sure, but I don't want to see it go...
Bought a game and now wont be able to play it... let me host my own private servers!

if amazon ever releases another game you buy it you're crazy. In fact don't ever buy another mmo unless you have in writing that it will be playable and the property of the purchaser as long as you have in installed

We should get at least a 50% refund on this game if they will terminate it. 
If they had any respect towards their "clients" they would have sold the game to someone else to keep it alive.

Last time i played this game was dec 23, 2021. That being said i was hoping to try it once again in the future. I feel bad for folks who played thousands of hours just to find out the game will shut down. If only Amazon could sell this game to someone else who will resurrect it better then ever.

Imagine hearing from a friend you wanted to join you in this game, that they can't because the company is shutting down the servers. How truly sad, this game has been really fun to play. My husband is the one who got me into it.
Fudge Amazon.  Skum company and should be forced to refund everyones money for this garbage.

if you want to shut down the game that we all paid for, the least you should do is refund it. at least i didnt waste any more money on microtransactions..what a disgrace. Amazon Game Studios, I will never buy from you again. No sense of ethical responsiblity.

Solid game for what it was a unique take on an MMO, combat was a bit sloppy, But, resource gathering and crafting was honestly really fun. dungeons etc where also unique but you unlock them so late into the game i barely did them.

At the end of the day, sadly. amazon removing it just like that is a laugh to everyone's face. might be the new game meta. make an MMO people are forced to spend years and hours on then to just delete the servers in a MMO rug pull wasting everyone's time and money. Nothing happens to the company/refund etc in the long run because somewhere in a policy agreement its legally fine and Jeff Bezos is daddy. laughable.

I had fun playing this game until I didn't. By the time I would ever feel like doing another proper playthrough it will have been long dead. Some of the game is fun and some of it is humourus or dramatic... but all of it is grindy.
5/10

New World is a GREAT MMO. I truly wish I could recommend it. Unfortunately, Amazon Game Studios pulled the plug on the developers, basically 20 days after a very successful expansion release. I hope it finds new life in some way. For right now, [b][u][i]DO NOT BUY.[/i][/u][/b]

I personally do not trust AGS with any live service game after this. If it something I am expecting will get more content or updates to add value to my purchase, I will not be buying it from AGS. They screwed the people working on the dev team, and the players in a major way here and don't deserve support.

Was it a good game? Debatable. It's certainly been a point of contention. I, for one, enjoyed my time playing. However, what I don't enjoy is companies being able to shut games down after building hype and taking people's money, only to then take that money and announce that the game will be taken offline. There's a term for that - rug pull. Continuing to offer items in the in-game store after announcing that the game is being shuttered? Not a good look. This isn't even the first time Scamazon has done this with a game, either. There is a noticeable pattern going on here. At this point they shouldn't be allowed to release games. They're obviously incapable of doing so with any kind of honesty or integrity.

most fun i had from this game was trolling people as i gathered resources to stop them from farming it so i could farm people. 

after i gathered all mats and crafted the elite armor i was completely bored and stopped playing 5/10

I started my adventure with this game in beta all those years ago with the initial release. I had its ups and downs but overall it was a lovely world with lots to explore. When the new expansion came out I was super stoked I bought the previous expansion and the new one even bought it for my husband. We were enjoying it then they announced the game would be shutting down. Why would they do that after a huge success they went from 12k players to 51k that screams people want to play your game. I feel like every game I touch dies at this point and I hope that Amazon realizes that they have killed a good game and PLEASE new touch a another video game again it has so much potential and I can't even get a refund for my husband who played less than 10 hours before the announcement that it was shutting down. RIP New World you deserved better and so did we.

Me and one of my friends really liked it sad to see it go. My other friends say they would goon to it and when they weren't satisfied they uninstalled it. I personally thought it was satisfying enough but to each their own. I once played Resident Evil 2 and never could get out of the police station because i got tired of the retracing steps and going back and fourth all the time. It was also slow and Leon Kennedy didn't moan enough for me.

This game is a bit of a mixed bag for me, on one hand the story is servicable and engaging however the crafting can be a tremendus grind. The latest Nighthaven update also has downright bad voice acting at times while the rest of the game is rock solid VA wise. The combat is a highlight, theres alot of nuance and I imagine wars and other pvp events are amazing once I get the chance to try them.  Despite this I can see the potential in this game and I am eagar to see where the developers take the gameplay and story in the coming years of its service due to the fact that Jeof Bezas has never abandoned a project and never will. 6.5/10

Truly the entire Playbook of rushed release into not listening to any community feedback to shutdown 4 years after launch. The moment we saw the Amazon logo, we should all have known that the only thing that company ever stood for was ruining industries, quick money and a staggering disregard to the future. 

I would love to give the entire Studio the Review instead of New World, because on a fundamental level this could have really been the new big thing, gameplay, world, vibes, mechanics, it was all there, but the issues were so glaring and clear from the very beginning that its a miracle we even made it to the 4 year mark. 

Story was atrociously paced on release, with the game straight up not being finished post level 35 or something. Enemies had wild amounts of respawn rate and aggro range, the performance of the game somehow got worse and worse with every patch too. I remember being able to play this easily at 60fps at launch (apart from server hiccups) and by now I somehow hit 25 fps at most in some areas, all with the same PC and parts as it was 4 years ago, without noticable performance loss in any other games. 

Then the PVP balance, the game never got that right as well, and regular server reboots because one of the 3 factions completely overtook a server, because they somehow never limited the amount of Areas a faction can claim, which to this day I think would have fixed the entire Siege/Pvp mechanics ever. 

If you ever took extended breaks, you can be certain that you have been completely left behind by those playing regularly. Time gating, limits and otherwise common developer misbehaviour were also a mainstay. 

Tldr.: Holy wasted potential, this could have been it but it got Amazon´t

Wow, i was interested in this game and enjoyed the combat for a bit and the leveling but honestly it was quite boring, staying within a few cities with nothing other than work to do. Quests sorely lacking. Town economy systems were interesting at first but with people able to just extract money out, just pilfering, wild, it devolved quickly. fighting animals was interesting, but rare flying enemies or anything too difficult. Some areas had just a lot of enemies expecting large groups so you could make little progress, then a group rolls by and suddenly the place is overrun but farming and camping isnt rewarding or interesting. Some of the resource things had potential, but it didnt quite make it. Fishing was fun, but potion making and crafting and fighting just felt half baked, just interesting enough, but would flop like how pvp was just axe invincible smashes rolling everyone. Dungeons werent anything special and most lore was forgettable. They could've shined it up more, worked on it, made it a smaller area with better progressions or more distinct ideas and quests but nah. hyped up the markets but them and the towns got tedious just standing around trading. Now the games dead. Let the community open servers! Stop killing games!
Game is abandoned because the Devs ruined it early on in development too bad such a great start.
Good graphics, good audio, good combat, good crafting. Overall fun to play. Yet to get to end game. Will update my comment if I do.
Steam denied my refund twice even tho this crap is shut down, I know Amazon is to blame but still I'm frustrated!

(resharing here my post from fb last Oct 30, 2025 just to pay tribute to one of the best games I've ever played)
Still mourning 😭😭😭
They're shutting down the world where I've built friendships across expeditions, catacomb runs, daily quests, and faction wars.
When real life got heavy, Aeternum became my safe place — my escape, my second home. It hurts knowing that no more updates will come — that the world my friends and I once roamed is now slowly falling into silence.
Now, I can't even open the game without getting emotional...
How do you patch a broken heart, Amazon? 💔💔💔

Imagine a trillion dollar company, not having the money to keep servers up. Please blacklist Amazon from Steam.

♥♥♥♥ Bezos

Game delisted and no longer available for purchase and will be permanently taken offline so no one can play it, but you can still do microtransactions cause these greedy pigs haven't stolen enough already. scum of the earth

The first MMO that I actually enjoyed I made some good memoires staying up late at night grinding for new gear. Sitting right outside Windsward doing the dungeon on release. Remembering how I wish I'd gotten the first Twitch drop. Complaining about how the map is so big and how we had no mounts to ride on and running for 30 minutes to get to a new location exploring every cave looking up guides on the best mining route to increase crafting skills. Meeting new people starting a clan seeing it grow and taking over your first territory it was amazing...Sad to see it go and how they made something so great and they let it die. New world wasn't perfect at release I know that, but it had so much potential it was a beautiful game. I'll always be thankful for it and I'm still looking for an MMO that gave me the same feeling that this game gave me.

Memory when it launched. Mangalor (original launch server) running around with the boys questing with pvp on cause everyone is new, some purple guy (plsdonthurtme) talking trash in voip at Hermits Shrine HS (rip), trashed our boys drip, we fought him one at a time, he sucked, called his boys, 8v8 bloody deathmatch at HS, more purple and green join in the festivities. eventually outnumbered by purple, put out SOS in faction chat, ole boy (DannyDeVito) said "the minutemen are coming". minutes pass, hear "AIM, FIRE" and dozen muskets go off behind us into the purple thugs. turn around to see an american revolution cosplay group sprint down hill with hatchets. fight for another hour. sleep. wake up next day and every single territory is at war, world war one lasted 4 irl weeks, before gold patch, crashed economic and social structures in server. dope
Estuvo bueno mientras duro, los servers murieron despues de la pandemia o por lomenos en Latinoamerica

♥♥♥♥ game. wasn't even good when I played it. Felt oddly grindy and I had no clue when I would progress past the first area so I never did. Game lost me after less than 20 hours. Worst 60€ I have spent. Now I come to hear that servers are shutting down completely. Take back this garbage from me and give me my money back. Least you can do is allow me to remove this parasite from my Steam library somehow.

Gloria victis you can do pvp all the time and have special state of war as its called ingame of your own. But New world is never try to be a huge theme park mmo. The devs were smoking too much at work and thought it be a good idea to switch all the pvp elements to be more pve storyline. Which won't help what so ever. You can be black desert but you never be black desert when it comes to mix pvp and pve theme park mmo.

Although I truly didn't play as much as I should've, New World gave me the kind of dopamine that no other RPG game has before. The systems and playstyle that the game provides is truly one of a kind. I hope and pray that the people who are working towards an offline version succeed because this game, although not appreciated enough by me, was amazing. Truly screw Amazon for delisting a game for "not a large enough player base" as a multi trillion dollar company. Maybe just maybe, they'll say it was just one big elaborate April Fools prank..

I really want to go back to this game before it shutdowns, but every time that I want to play this game again, the game says, "No! No- I'm going to crash your PC- or at least try my best to! Aghh!" 

Even so, I'm sure there's a solution. I'm just not interested in the title (That's a lie, but I can't get myself to reinstall).

I'm glad that I chose to watch paint dry over the years, instead! <3
